title: rLens Source Acquisition Report (source-acquisition-report v1.0)
description: Machine-readable provenance/diagnostic report for rLens source acquisition (remote_snapshot mode). It records how the scanned content was acquired and whether any local repo was mutated. This artifact is a provenance/diagnostic signal, not a truth verdict: it describes structure and provenance, not that the snapshot is byte-identical to a locally generated bundle. remote_snapshot never mutates the local repo, so every repo entry reports local_repo_mutated=false.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of report creation.

hub.description: Resolved hub path the job ran against.

mode.description: Source acquisition mode label for the report. v1 writes this report only for remote_snapshot.

plan_only.description: Whether the job was a dry-plan (ref resolution only; no materialization or scan).

remote_ref_policy.description: Ref-selection policy applied when no explicit remote_ref was given.

requested_remote_ref.description: Explicit remote_ref requested by the client, if any.

repo.description: Repository name (derived from the original local path).

original_path.description: Original local repo path (provenance); never the scanned snapshot path.

scan_path.description: Path actually scanned (the isolated snapshot dir), or null for plan-only/failed entries.

resolved_ref.description: Display name of the resolved ref (e.g. origin/main, refs/tags/v1).

remote_url_redacted.description: Remote URL with embedded credentials redacted. Never contains userinfo secrets.

local_repo_mutated.description: Always false for remote_snapshot: the local repo is never fetched into, merged, checked out, switched or reset.

stderr.description: Credential-redacted git stderr for failures, if any.
